touches.ai_summary: Ava Gomez from SagePrism submitted an inbound request expressing interest in CRM automation solutions for their RevOps team. Initial inquiry focused on workflow automation capabilities.
next_steps: Send follow-up email with overview of automation capabilities
Schedule qualification call

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent to Ava providing overview of CRM automation platform and positioning against market alternatives. Highlighted RevOps-specific use cases.

questions_asked.question: How does your solution compare to Clari for forecasting accuracy?
questions_asked.answer: Positioned our automation-first approach vs Clari's analytics focus, noting complementary use cases

next_steps: Schedule qualification call to understand current process pain points

touches.ai_summary: Ava confirmed availability for qualification call and shared preliminary team size (around 20-25 RevOps/Sales users). Expressed urgency around Q2 planning cycle.
next_steps: Qualification call scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: 16-minute qualification call with Ava. Discussed current CRM workflow challenges, manual data entry burden on sales team, and need for better pipeline visibility. Ava mentioned evaluating Clari but concerned about complexity. Team size confirmed at 22 seats across RevOps and Sales.

questions_asked.question: What's typical implementation timeline for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: 4-6 weeks for core automation setup, with consulting engagement to customize workflows

questions_asked.question: Who else would be involved in evaluating this solution?
questions_asked.answer: Ava mentioned Aria Silva (RevOps Analyst) and potentially IT for technical review

next_steps: Schedule discovery call to map current workflows
Send use case examples for RevOps teams

touches.ai_summary: 27-minute discovery call with Ava diving into SagePrism's current RevOps processes. Identified key pain points: duplicate data entry, inconsistent pipeline updates, and lack of automation triggers. Ava expressed strong interest in automations tier but noted budget discussion needed with leadership. Mentioned Clari again as alternative being considered.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform integrate with our existing Salesforce instance and Slack?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, confirmed native integrations for both with workflow automation across tools

questions_asked.question: What level of technical resources do we need on our side for implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Minimal - consulting package includes configuration and training, IT involvement for SSO/security review

questions_asked.question: What's driving the timeline for this decision?
questions_asked.answer: Q2 planning cycle and need to reduce manual workload before summer peak season

objections: Budget authority unclear - needs CFO or executive approval for software purchases over $15K
next_steps: Map out technical requirements with IT team
Prepare demo focused on automation workflows
Discuss pricing and package options

touches.ai_summary: Sent follow-up email to Ava with technical requirements checklist and requested introduction to IT contact for security/integration review. Included pricing calculator for 22 seats with automation tier.
next_steps: Connect with Jackson Hill (IT Director) for technical evaluation
Schedule demo for broader team

touches.ai_summary: Ava responded introducing Jackson Hill from IT and Aria Silva from her team. Confirmed interest in demo and requested comparison document vs Clari since they're evaluating both options. Noted that procurement will also need to be involved (Ethan Ryan).

questions_asked.question: Can you provide a side-by-side feature comparison with Clari?
questions_asked.answer: Will prepare competitive comparison focused on automation capabilities and implementation complexity

next_steps: Send Clari comparison document
Schedule multi-stakeholder demo

touches.ai_summary: 14-minute follow-up call with Ava to discuss demo logistics and stakeholder alignment. Ava mentioned that executive sponsor engagement has been challenging - CFO is focused on other priorities. Discussed competitive positioning vs Clari and confirmed automation use cases are the primary differentiator.

questions_asked.question: Is there an executive champion we should include in upcoming conversations?
questions_asked.answer: Ava noted she reports to VP Sales but they haven't been engaged yet on this evaluation

questions_asked.question: What's your flexibility on pricing for a multi-year commitment?
questions_asked.answer: Discussed volume discounts and noted pricing will depend on final package selection

next_steps: Demo scheduled with Ava, Aria, Jackson, and account managers
Prepare ROI analysis for executive presentation

touches.ai_summary: Post-demo follow-up email sent to all attendees. Demo covered core automation workflows, Salesforce integration, and reporting capabilities. Team seemed engaged, particularly around automated pipeline updates and forecast accuracy features. Jackson raised questions about data security and SOC2 compliance.
next_steps: Provide SOC2 documentation and security questionnaire responses to Jackson
Schedule technical deep-dive for IT review

touches.ai_summary: Ava requested formal proposal with pricing for 22 seats, automations tier plus consulting package. Mentioned budget target is around $12-15K annually. Also asked for customer references in similar RevOps use cases.

questions_asked.question: Do you have reference customers in SaaS with similar team sizes?
questions_asked.answer: Provided three reference contacts from mid-market SaaS companies using automation tier

next_steps: Prepare formal proposal with pricing options
Facilitate reference calls

touches.ai_summary: 23-minute meeting with Ava and Megan Mitchell (Account Manager) to review preliminary proposal. Discussed $17.8K pricing for 22 seats with consulting package. Megan raised concerns about pricing being higher than expected. Ava reiterated budget constraints and mentioned Clari pricing came in lower for similar capabilities.

questions_asked.question: Is there room to negotiate on the consulting package cost?
questions_asked.answer: Noted flexibility to adjust scope or phase implementation to manage costs

questions_asked.question: What's the decision-making process from here?
questions_asked.answer: Needs procurement review, IT security sign-off, and informal CFO approval

objections: Pricing concern - initial quote exceeds budget expectations
Competitive pressure from Clari's lower pricing
next_steps: Revise proposal with pricing options
Schedule follow-up to address procurement requirements

touches.ai_summary: Sent revised proposal with three pricing tiers: base automation ($13K), automation plus consulting ($17.8K), and premium with expanded support ($22K). Highlighted ROI analysis showing 6-month payback on mid-tier option.
next_steps: Review proposal options with Ava and procurement team
Address any outstanding technical questions from Jackson

touches.ai_summary: Ava responded that mid-tier option ($17.8K) aligns best with requirements but needs approval on pricing. Ethan Ryan from procurement copied on thread requesting vendor information form and contract terms for review. Jackson confirmed security review is progressing.
next_steps: Complete procurement vendor questionnaire
Provide standard contract terms to Ethan

touches.ai_summary: Submitted vendor questionnaire and contract terms to Ethan. Ava mentioned they're nearing end of fiscal quarter and asked about any additional flexibility on pricing to close before month-end. Reiterated that Clari is still in consideration and pricing is competitive factor.
next_steps: Discuss pricing flexibility with leadership
Schedule proposal review call to address final questions

touches.ai_summary: 26-minute proposal review call with Ava, Aria, and Ethan. Discussed contract terms, implementation timeline, and pricing. Ethan raised questions about payment terms and renewal conditions. Ava pushed for discount to meet budget threshold. Discussed 26% discount to bring total to $13K, matching their budget target.

questions_asked.question: Can we structure payment as quarterly rather than annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Standard terms are annual, but can discuss payment plan options

questions_asked.question: What happens to pricing at renewal if we want to add seats?
questions_asked.answer: Renewal pricing locked for term, seat additions prorated at same rate

objections: Pricing still above budget even with standard discount
Payment terms preference for quarterly vs annual
next_steps: Seek approval for extended discount to $13K
Prepare final contract with agreed terms

touches.ai_summary: 24-minute meeting with Ava and Aria to finalize terms. Confirmed 26.9% discount to reach $13K total, though this reduces margin significantly. Ava committed to moving forward pending final IT security sign-off from Jackson and informal CFO nod. No executive sponsor formally engaged in evaluation.

questions_asked.question: Should we include VP Sales or CFO in final approval discussion?
questions_asked.answer: Ava will handle internal approvals, prefers to keep current stakeholder group

next_steps: Finalize contract at $13K pricing
Obtain IT security final approval
Target signature before quarter-end

touches.ai_summary: 38-minute negotiation call with Ava, Ethan, and Aria to review final contract. Ethan raised concerns about auto-renewal clause and requested opt-in renewal instead. Discussed data retention and termination terms. Ava confirmed budget approved at $13K level but emphasized need to close quickly before end of month.

questions_asked.question: Can we modify auto-renewal to require explicit opt-in at end of term?
questions_asked.answer: Standard practice is auto-renewal with 30-day notice, but can discuss exceptions

questions_asked.question: What's the data export process if we decide not to renew?
questions_asked.answer: Full data export available in standard formats, 30-day retention post-termination

questions_asked.question: Is Jackson's security review complete?
questions_asked.answer: Final approval expected this week, no major concerns identified

objections: Timeline pressure creating urgency but also scrutiny
next_steps: Revise contract with opt-in renewal language
Confirm Jackson's final security approval
Prepare for signature

touches.ai_summary: 41-minute negotiation session with expanded group including Megan Mitchell. Reviewed revised contract with opt-in renewal terms. Ethan requested additional language around implementation deliverables and success criteria for consulting package. Megan expressed concerns about support SLAs and escalation process. Jackson joined briefly to confirm security approval complete.

questions_asked.question: What guarantees do we have around implementation timeline and deliverables?
questions_asked.answer: Standard SOW includes milestone timeline and acceptance criteria, can formalize in contract appendix

questions_asked.question: What are support response times for critical issues?
questions_asked.answer: Business tier includes 4-hour response for P1 issues, 24-hour for P2, detailed in SLA appendix

questions_asked.question: Are we aligned on quarter-end signature target?
questions_asked.answer: Ava confirmed working toward this week, pending final procurement review

objections: Implementation deliverables need clearer definition in contract
next_steps: Add implementation SOW appendix to contract
Provide detailed SLA documentation
Final procurement review

touches.ai_summary: 24-minute final review with Ava, Ethan, and Aria. Contract updated with all requested terms: opt-in renewal, implementation SOW appendix, SLA documentation. Ethan confirmed procurement approval. Ava noted CFO informally aware but not directly engaged in decision. Agreed to move forward with signature, though still competitive tension with Clari as backup option. Deal positioned to close at $13K (26.9% discount from list).

questions_asked.question: Do you need any final approvals before signing?
questions_asked.answer: Ava has authority to sign, CFO informed of spend level

questions_asked.question: When can implementation kick off after signature?
questions_asked.answer: Onboarding team can start within 5 business days of signed contract

next_steps: Send final contract for signature
Schedule implementation kickoff
Assign customer success manager
